checks.finding: Every AC reference in tasks.md resolves to AC-001 through AC-007 in requirements.md and to the corresponding TEST-001 through TEST-006 evidence in acceptance-tests.md.
All eight tasks declare Blockers as either None or comma-separated T-NNN identifiers; blockers_format_valid is true in precheck-result.json.
T-001 through T-008 collectively cover every requirement from REQ-001 through REQ-011, consistent with traceability.md Task Mapping.

AC-001 through AC-007 are covered: T-001/T-002 cover AC-001 with distinct capability and routing scopes, T-004/T-005 cover distinct AC-002 implementation and review scopes, and T-003, T-006, T-007, and T-008 cover AC-003/004, AC-005, AC-006, and AC-007 respectively.
Every task T-001 through T-008 references at least one REQ-NNN in its Requirements field.
No task is an exclusively test-file task or has a test/spec/verify title; all tasks nevertheless reference their applicable AC-NNN identifiers.
In attempt 2 round 1, every task remains Approval: Draft and Status: Planned; no task is self-approved.
T-001, T-006, T-007, and T-008 are medium/acceptance-first; T-002 through T-005 are high/tdd. precheck-result.json records workflow_match_precheck as PASS.

Shared AC references have differentiated scopes: T-001 versus T-002 separates capability floors from routing/escalation for AC-001, while T-004 versus T-005 separates implementation-agent isolation from reviewer/evaluator isolation for AC-002.
Every blocker referenced by T-002 through T-008 exists in the dependency-graph.json nodes array, which contains T-001 through T-008.
The dependency graph is acyclic: dependencies flow from T-008 to T-007, through T-006 and the routing/isolation tasks, and terminate at T-001.

Each task has one coherent concern: capability tiers, routing/resume, manifest handoff, implementation contexts, reviewer isolation, reporting metrics, release synchronization, or rollback transaction.

Every task names inspectable tests, files, diagnostics, logs, reports, hashes, or validator outcomes. High-risk T-002 through T-005 additionally name red/green logs and independent-review report evidence.

traceability.md contains T-001 through T-008 and REQ-001 through REQ-011 without dangling identifiers. REQ-004 through REQ-007 use security-spec.md#security-spec, REQ-008 through REQ-011 use infra-spec.md#infrastructure-spec, and REQ-001 through REQ-003 provide reasoned cross-layer N/A entries.
